title: Aluminium faces 'black swan' supply shock, Mercuria says
summary: Mercuria says the global aluminium market is facing a rare supply shock tied to Middle East war disruptions, with shortages expected to deepen this year. The report says the market could see at least a two-million-ton deficit by year-end, highlighting strain on production and capacity.

title: Latest Aluminium News
summary: NewsNow’s aluminium feed highlights several recent items related to production and capacity, including Rio Tinto’s Q1 2026 production increase and reports of shutdowns or cuts at major smelters. It also features market commentary on supply squeezes, pricing, and output caps affecting the industry.

title: Powering Up American Aluminum: A Roadmap for Next Generation ...
summary: This industry roadmap notes that domestic primary aluminum production has declined over recent decades, while recycled aluminum production has grown to record levels in 2024. It focuses on capacity, supply-chain resilience, and pathways to strengthen U.S. aluminum production.

title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
date: Current market page, updated within the past 24 hours
summary: Trading Economics reports aluminium prices at multi-year highs, citing prolonged supply disruptions from the Middle East as a key driver. The update ties the rally to trade and shipping disruptions affecting global supply and production flows.

title: News & Insights - Aluminum Market Update
date: Recent Q1 coverage on the page
summary: CRU’s news page includes aluminum market updates such as Hydro’s Q1 results, where higher prices supported margins despite weaker North American extrusion demand. The item is relevant to current production trends, capacity utilization, and regional demand conditions.

title: Ball Corporation reports record 2025 results as aluminium sustainability metrics advance
summary: Ball Corporation says its 2025 annual and sustainability report shows both record financial results and progress on environmental goals. The company reported that 74% of the aluminum used in its global beverage packaging business came from recycled sources in 2025, highlighting ongoing sustainability improvements in aluminum packaging.

title: Found Industries aims to strengthen America's industrial supply chains
summary: MIT News profiles Found Industries and its Found Energy division, which is advancing aluminum fuel technology. The company is running a 100-kilowatt-class demonstration plant and developing a catalyzed aluminum-water reactor designed to produce industrial heat and electricity while enabling recycling of the byproduct back into aluminum.

title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Steel prices fell to 3,211 CNY/T on May 15, down 0.86% from the previous day. The decline was linked to expectations of seasonal demand slowdown and softer downstream consumption, which weighed on sentiment despite recent gains over the past month.

title: HRC Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Hot-rolled coil steel rose to 1,135 USD/T, its highest level since January 2024. The move was supported by stronger demand expectations, stable Chinese production, and improved mill margins, which helped lift market sentiment.

title: US flat steel price edges higher again, May scrap now adds to bullish fundamentals
summary: SteelOrbis reported that US flat steel prices edged higher again as May scrap prices reinforced bullish fundamentals. The update suggests the market remains supported by firm pricing conditions and positive momentum in flat-rolled steel.

title: Daily Steel Prices
summary: Platts’ Steel Market Daily provides daily coverage of steel-related benchmarks and market pricing, including iron ore, scrap metal, and metallurgical coal. It is a key source for tracking broader steel market trends and price movements.

title: Global Iron and Steel Prices and News 18.05.2026
summary: SteelRadar’s latest daily news page highlights recent developments in the global iron and steel market. The page aggregates industry updates, pricing news, and commentary, but the snippet available does not show a specific article headline focused on production or capacity changes.

title: Industry Data - American Iron and Steel Institute
summary: AISI’s weekly update reports U.S. domestic raw steel production at 1,880,000 net tons for the week ending May 9, 2026, with a capability utilization rate of 81.4%. This represents an increase from 1,720,000 net tons and 76.6% utilization in the same week last year, indicating stronger output and higher plant use.

title: Copper extends retreat on faster inflation and stronger dollar
summary: Copper prices fell as faster U.S. inflation and a stronger dollar pressured industrial metals. The move added to recent weakness after a run-up to record levels, as traders reassessed demand and macroeconomic risks.

title: Copper prices surge to record $6.5 per pound amid tight supply and AI-fueled demand
summary: Copper surged to new highs on concerns about tight supply and rising demand linked to AI-related infrastructure growth. The article highlights how market participants remain focused on structural demand even after the rally.

title: Copper hits record $6.39 as Chile faces Codelco audit scandal
summary: Copper touched record levels as supply concerns in Chile added to already elevated market volatility. The report points to operational and governance issues at major producer Codelco as a factor supporting prices.

title: Copper: Tariff risk fuels rally
summary: Copper prices remained supported by trade-policy concerns, with tariff risk contributing to bullish sentiment. The piece suggests market participants are pricing in continued supply and policy uncertainty.

title: Aluminium recycling sector flags EU ETS risks, surge in copper prices too
summary: The article notes that rising copper prices are affecting broader metals and recycling markets. It also connects the move to policy and cost pressures in Europe, where emissions-related risks are influencing industrial inputs.

title: Chilean miner Codelco’s El Teniente smelter to restart after collapse
summary: Codelco said its El Teniente smelter is set to restart after a collapse-related disruption. The restart could ease some near-term supply concerns, though the market remains sensitive to production risks in Chile.

title: Copper futures drop amid Chilean mine cuts and AI demand
summary: Copper futures fell about 1% even as supply concerns intensified. Cochilco data showed output declines at Chile’s three largest mines, with Codelco, Escondida, and Collahuasi together producing roughly 43,000 fewer metric tons year over year, while sulfuric acid shortages are adding further pressure on producers.

title: Global Copper Shortage: AI Demand vs Physical Supply Limits
summary: This report argues that copper supply is being squeezed by structural mining disruptions just as demand rises from AI data centers, EVs, and wind turbines. It highlights production problems at major mines, including disruptions in Indonesia and persistent weakness at Codelco, alongside sharply higher copper prices.

title: Copper Market News & Forecasts
summary: Fastmarkets reports that the copper market is being shaped by tight mine supply and record-high prices, with analysts warning of unsustainable deficits. The coverage also notes major production changes, including strong African output at Glencore and a steep drop in Ivanhoe Mines’ Kamoa-Kakula copper production.

title: Innovations to close the copper supply-demand gap.
summary: This article discusses how new technologies and supportive policies will be necessary to address the looming copper supply-demand gap. It highlights projected demand growth and warns that a significant shortfall could hinder electrification and emissions-reduction goals.

title: Rewiring a sustainable, clean energy future with copper
summary: The article explains copper's critical role in enabling a net-zero energy system because of its efficiency in transmitting electricity. It notes that copper demand for clean energy could nearly double by 2050, driven by EVs and renewable power expansion.

title: Sustainable and efficient operations for copper miners
summary: EY outlines how copper miners are responding to strong clean-energy demand and supply deficits by investing in efficiency, digitalization, and lower-carbon operations. The piece also highlights responsible mining, regulatory challenges, and the push for low-carbon copper products.

title: Copper in the Age of AI: Challenges of Electrification
summary: This report examines how AI, data centers, electrification, and the energy transition are shaping copper demand through 2040. It emphasizes the need for faster mine development and expanded processing capacity, alongside policy and technological innovation.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Nickel prices rose to around $18,605.50 per tonne on May 18, 2026, though they pulled back from a recent two-year high near $19,600 as traders took profits. The move reflects a pause in momentum rather than weakening fundamentals, with supply disruptions and tighter raw material availability still supporting the market.

title: Nickel Prices Today 2026 | Live Chart, Battery Grade & Stainless Steel
summary: Nickel was reported edging lower on the day after earlier gains, reflecting short-term profit-taking in a market that has recently been driven higher by supply constraints. The article highlights Indonesia's reduced mining quotas and a sulfur shortage that could tighten downstream processing for battery-grade nickel.

title: Nickel Price (Monthly) - Historical Data & Trends
summary: YCharts shows nickel at 17,961.60 USD/mt for April 2026, up from both the prior month and a year earlier. The data suggests continued upward momentum in the nickel market over the medium term.

title: Global price of Nickel (PNICKUSDM)
summary: FRED's monthly series shows the global nickel price at 17,076.29 in March 2026, below January but above levels from late 2025. The series provides a broader context for recent market moves and confirms that nickel prices have remained elevated relative to a year ago.

title: Nickel's Long-Term Bull Cycle Gains Momentum Amid Supply Management Shifts
summary: This video discusses how the global nickel market is holding in a narrow price range, supported by tighter supply management, especially in Indonesia. It also highlights Canada Nickel's permitting progress and broader industry developments that could affect future nickel production and capacity.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Trading Economics reports that nickel prices strengthened as supply disruptions and tighter raw material availability supported the market. The article notes production constraints in Indonesia and additional processing pressures affecting nickel output.

title: Nickel Headed to $20,000 as Indonesia Limits Production, EV Demand Surges
summary: This market update says nickel prices are being supported by Indonesia's reduced ore production targets and improving EV demand. It also discusses how supply discipline in Indonesia is becoming the key factor influencing near-term nickel pricing and capacity expectations.

title: Refining Power
summary: C4ADS examines Indonesia's nickel refining sector and finds that Chinese stakeholders control a large share of refining capacity. The piece focuses on how this concentration shapes global nickel production and processing power.

title: Nickel Industry News, Views and Commentary - Chronickels
summary: This blog aggregates recent nickel-related commentary on sustainability, treatment processes, and innovations in nickel production. It highlights topics such as low-waste cathode synthesis, green hydrogen, nickel-containing stainless steel, and regulatory developments affecting recycling and critical raw materials.

title: Nickel
summary: This coverage focuses on the latest nickel supply chain news and analysis from global experts. It emphasizes innovative ways to produce and use nickel as an energy metal for electric vehicles and energy storage.

title: Innovations in Sustainable Nickel Production: The Path to Low-Cost Solutions
summary: This article discusses rapid changes in the global nickel market, with an emphasis on innovations in mining and refining that aim to lower costs and improve environmental performance. It centers on sustainable production methods and process improvements in the nickel industry.

title: 24 hours with... Nickel Industries sustainability head Muchtazar
summary: This interview examines sustainability challenges in Indonesia's fast-growing nickel sector, driven by surging EV battery demand. It also explores how the industry is responding to scrutiny over environmental and social impacts.

title: Nickel Market Dynamics and the Security of the Battery Supply Chain
summary: This memo outlines recent nickel market developments and the growing importance of nickel in battery supply chains. It notes rapid demand growth from battery cathodes and projects substantial long-term increases in nickel demand under clean energy scenarios.

title: Tenaris acquires Artrom Steel Tubes for EUR86 million
summary: Tenaris announced a definitive agreement to acquire 100% of Artrom Steel Tubes SA for EUR86 million on a cash-free and debt-free basis. Artrom is a steel tubes company, and the deal is expected to close in the fourth quarter of 2026. This is the most relevant recent item tying Tenaris to steel operations in Italy/Europe in the past day.

title: Tenaris to acquire Artrom Steel Tubes S.A. seamless pipe manufacturing plant in Romania
summary: Tenaris published the official announcement of its agreement to acquire Artrom Steel Tubes S.A. from GLGH Steel, LLC. The company describes Artrom as a seamless pipe manufacturing plant in Romania, reinforcing its European industrial footprint.

title: Prysmian S.p.A. stock (IT0004176001): cable champion in focus after strong 2024 results and guidance update
summary: This article says Prysmian has drawn renewed investor attention after strong full-year 2024 results and a March 2025 guidance update. It highlights the company’s position as a major global cable group and references a management conference call following the update.

title: Prysmian Daily News Update
summary: The latest update mentions Prysmian’s criticism of the exclusion of cables from the EU’s proposed Industrial Acceleration Act. It appears to be an internal or branded news bulletin covering company developments and related industry trends.
